reasoning: The question asks whether the number of protests in the 30 days prior to the resolution date will exceed the 360-day average of 128.25. While domestic political reckoning and potential post-war inquiries in Israel could trigger significant protests, social mobilizations generally exhibit mean reversion over time. Because the baseline 360-day average encompasses very high-intensity periods of wartime and hostage demonstrations, a future distinct 30-day period is slightly more likely to fall below this elevated average rather than exceed it, assuming acute crises stabilize. Taking into account the high variance of 30-day periods, I estimate the probability at 45%.
